reviews.review_msg: He's gotten a lot better. The previous reviews are about his time teaching. He's far more organised and practical now. The grading is still strict, but he's a really sweet guy. Happens to be pretty handsome as well. Would definitely take another class with him.
Steve was an interesting prof. He does know his content well and we only had 4 assignments for the whole term which were generally easy.

Incredibly interesting as an academic and I've taken multiple classes of his for this reason but it doesn't seem like cares much about his role as an educator. He's very ambitious in wanting students to produce very novel/profound reflections on theory is therefore excessively harsh in his grading and would likely be better suited to research.

One of the worst professors in communications department, unorganized & definitely doesn't know what he is doing. Very clear that he is just there for a paycheque & does not care if you succeed. Told us to give him high ratings on here in class. Ends class early.

cancelled classes constantly. was very unreliable when asked for help outside of class. It felt like he was confused about the syllabus and it was very unorganized. He would end lectures super early. He would just trail off, not get back on track, then end class. strange guy and terrible lecturer

Everyone saying he's a bad prof for cancelling classes during Fall 2023 are ridiculous. It was annoying that so many classes were cancelled but he was just respecting the TA strike. 

I do wish he had prepared a bit more for the class, but considering it was his first time teaching it, it was pretty good and he was super nice and approachable.
Amazing professor, his lectures are super engaging and interesting. Also, the syllabus is so good and it was divided very equally. I love it!

Steven is so passionate in his lectures. He has a tendency to be a bit all over the place but you learn so much if you pay attention and get involved. His office hours are also super helpful and he'll stay so late after lecture to help out his students. He really cares about all of his students and wants them to do well in his class!
never take this course with him! He is confusing and also a tough grader. His TA doesn't look like they want to teach the tutorial. Didn't learn anything from this course.
worst professor ever. Only reads from the PPT and a very tough grader.

GENUINELY the most unprepared disorganized teacher ever... I am beyond angry that we had to pay full tuition while he just cancelled more than half the lectures without reason. He is the definition of a privileged American white man. Super condescending and is of no help when you ask him anything.

Prof is a very personable guy, probably great outside of class but as a professor, he lacked. His lectures were all over the place, didn't follow the curriculum that he gave us so the readings we did for the week weren't relevant to that lecture. Marks were based on 4 things of which 3 were due last day of class.

If his teaching attitude is so unserious, he can choose not to be a professor. He is the worst professor I have ever met at SFU. He seems very nice but...He still canceled half of the semester's classes due to the strike and screwed our grade.

He is very irresponsible as a professor. He often piles up things from the previous class to the next class. The workload of the entire semester is piled into the last week, not considering the fact that students have other finals to prepare for, the average score is only 50 to 60.He is attitude is very bad. Can't learn anything but has a GPA bomb.

the dude made a midterm exam, final exam, and a 2000 word paper due on the same day on the last week of class totaling 75% of our grade. Both exams were written essays as well. The TSSU strikes did screw things up this semester but this was not a good way to handle things as I and many others also had other finals to focus on. Everyone did bad lol

He showed up to class without even preparing his slides,canceled half the semester.He seems friendly,but when you ask him something,he doesn't know what he talking about and he's condescending.Also he's the definition of a privileged American white man.All the homework was piled up in the last week.He only read the slides,you can't learn anything

Didn't prepare and canceled half of his lectures, he put everything on the last two days, a mess. Difficult marker, He didn't tell the grades for each assignment and just ended up with a horrible final grade. He won't tell you why or give you comments. he's condescending. His lectures always ended an hour early, a horrible, unorganized professor.

Steven Malcic was a decent prof. All assignments are graded by the TA so your grade is dependent on whether or not they are a tough grader. Steven is a bit weird and has a strange approach to teaching. This was a writing heavy course so I don't recommend it if it's not mandatory for you.

Absolutely horrible. He didn't plan any of the course or assignments so was just making everything up as he went along. Result was everything: 2 exams and a final paper being crammed into the last day of class. He also cancelled about half the classes the day of with no explanation. Totally disorganized and incompetent and not fit to be a prof

Absolutely horrible. He didn't plan any of the course or assignments so was just making everything up as he went along. Result was everything: 2 exams and a final paper being crammed into the last day of class. He also cancelled about half the classes the day of with no explanation. Totally disorganized and incompetent and not fit to be a prof!

This class was not enjoyable. The lack of communication was absolutely horrible and 90% of the time no one knew what was going on (including the TA) in the lectures, assignments, and the take-home final. He often would cancel class with very little notice and would not change the syllabus, would just cram everything into another class.

Really enjoyed taking a class with Dr. Malcic. He creates a positive environment with engaging lectures. Would recommend taking a class with him at SFU. The overall content was interesting and the lectures were engaging.

If he's marking your stuff GOOD LUCK. He is such a difficult marker. Was the definition of a white American privileged man. He made snarky comments and was super condescending. He'll lecture something and say "yeah whatever" when he doesn't wanna explain. Assignments were SO long yet worth nothing. Midterm and Final were difficult. DON'T RECOMMEND.

Steve is a great prof. He really makes you want to come to class. He is not like any of the other stuffy profs in communication who are pretentious academics, he actually sees the importance within pop culture as an indication for now. Very modern. He also plays really good music before class!

I thought Steve seemed very nice, but it's evident that he cares more about being well-liked than actually supporting students' learning. Gave out tons of free marks that reward students who haven't done any work, and he's very dismissive outside of lecture. He's the epitome of a "chill" teacher who doesn't care about what you get out of the class.

It was refreshing taking a class with a teacher that wants to provide students with skills that are transferable for after school, not just in prep for a masters. His lectures were informative and pushed to get students to think critically about the material. It's obvious he has a passion for the subject matter. Always available for extra help.

Steven Malcic is one of the best professors I have ever had! His lectures are so relevant and interesting, he provides excellent feedback and his very quick to respond to emails, and genuinely wants his students to do well. I was pleasantly surprised to receive 100% on the take home final.

I had taken this class as Malcic had seemed very nice. However while in the class I found that when I would go to him for help outside of the classroom he was often hurtful and disrespectful to me. Most office visits I had left in tears and very anxious for the next class.

Steve was one of the best profs I've had throughout my 5 years at SFU. He genuinely cares about his students and is always willing to help. He made the 3-hour lectures entertaining and informative. He's funny and relatable. I look forward to having more classes with him.

Steven is without a doubt the best professor I've had across multiple schools in my 3+ years of university. I've never had a prof make himself so available to talk about anything and everything. He's a passionate, hilarious dude and I always looked forward to his lectures despite it being three hours and my last of three lectures on a Thursday.
